_callMintingFeeTracking(uint256,address,.details: This is called via try/catch to maintain compatibility with standard UBIFeeSplitter.

params.gusdAmount: Amount of gUSD to redeem (18 decimals)

params.usdcAmount: Amount of USDC to deposit (6 decimals)

_callMintingFeeTracking(uint256,address,.notice: Helper function to call enhanced minting fee tracking.         Attempts to use StableUBIFeeSplitter.splitMintingFee() if available.

acceptAdmin().notice: Step 2 of the two-step admin transfer.         Must be called by the previously proposed admin.

pendingAdmin().notice: Pending admin for the two-step admin transfer (GOO-493).

previewGUSDForUSDC(uint256).notice: Preview USDC out for a given gUSD input (after fee).

previewUSDCForGUSD(uint256).notice: Preview gUSD out for a given USDC input (after fee).

swapGUSDForUSDC(uint256).notice: Swap gUSD for USDC.         User sends `gusdAmount` (18-decimal), receives USDC minus fee (6-decimal).

swapUSDCForGUSD(uint256).notice: Swap USDC for gUSD.         User sends `usdcAmount` (6-decimal), receives gUSD minus fee (18-decimal).

transferAdmin(address).notice: Step 1 of a two-step admin transfer (GOO-493).         A typo or compromised key cannot brick the contract because the         proposed admin must explicitly call `acceptAdmin`.

withdrawReserves(address,uint256).notice: Withdraw USDC reserves to a recipient (e.g., for yield strategy).         Cannot withdraw below the amount needed to back outstanding gUSD.

documentation.text: @dev Alias for readability — IgUSD has all needed methods

@notice Enhanced UBIFeeSplitter interface for PSM minting fee tracking.
         Only implemented by StableUBIFeeSplitter, not the standard UBIFeeSplitter.
@notice Pending admin for the two-step admin transfer (GOO-493).

@dev Gross gUSD ever minted by this PSM (never decremented on redemption).
      Used with psmBurnedGUSD to compute outstanding PSM-backed gUSD for
      the withdrawReserves solvency check.
      Note: The PSM accepts redemptions from ANY gUSD holder (including
      VaultManager-minted gUSD). We cannot distinguish origins at burn time,
      so psmMintedGUSD is conservatively kept as gross issuance. This means
      withdrawReserves may be more restrictive than necessary after cross-origin
      redemptions, but it guarantees admin cannot drain reserves while original
      PSM users still hold unredeemed gUSD.

@notice Step 2 of the two-step admin transfer.
         Must be called by the previously proposed admin.

@notice Withdraw USDC reserves to a recipient (e.g., for yield strategy).
         Cannot withdraw below the amount needed to back outstanding gUSD.
@notice Preview gUSD out for a given USDC input (after fee).
@notice Preview USDC out for a given gUSD input (after fee).
